Faults.message:
We're sorry about the power cut in the area, we can see this is not the first time this has happened and we apologise for the repeat issues. The power cut is affecting some properties in the area shown on the map, but not all. This is why you may notice some of your neighbours have power, but you don't. It is normal to see 1 in 3 homes affected. We are working with our engineers to fix the problem as quickly as possible, and well keep you updated here on PowerTrack.

We've received an update from our engineers, they have advised that routine fixes haven't worked as planned, and we need to go in for full repairs. We have resourced an underground specialist, who will be on site by 16:30. At this stage, we are expecting your power to return by 00:30 17/04/2026.

The power cut has been caused by a fault on our underground network. Issues like this do happen, and our engineers are very experienced at fixing them. We'll keep you updated along the way.

If you need more information, please send us a message on Whatsapp or call us on 105 and quote reference 'SZ7970'
Faults.name: GU47 Area

Faults.message:
We're sorry about the power cut in the area. The power cut is affecting 12 properties in the area shown on the map. We are working with our engineers to fix the problem as quickly as possible, and will keep you updated here on Power Track.

Our engineers are now on site and are working on repairs to our main overhead network. They have given us an update, and advised that the works are more complex and they need more time for repairs. They have advised that further teams are being resourced to assist with the repairs, and at this stage, we're aiming to have your power back on by 00:00 17/04/2026.

The power cut has been caused by a fault on our overhead network. Issues like this do happen, and our engineers are very experienced at fixing them. We'll keep you updated along the way.

If you need more information, please send us a message on Whatsapp or call us on 105 and quote reference 'SZ8201'
Faults.name: BA12 Area

Faults.message:
We're sorry about the power cut in the area. The power cut is affecting 55 properties in the area shown on the map. We are working with our engineers to fix the problem as quickly as possible, and will keep you updated here on Power Track.

Engineers have arrived in the area. They are working hard to fix the problem and will get your power back on as quickly as possible. At this stage, we're aiming to have your power back on by 02:00 17/04/2026. If this changes, we'll let you know.

We have had reports of damage to our network. When this happens, the power will automatically turn off to keep everyone safe. Our engineers know exactly where the problem is and are working hard to make the situation safe, repair the wire and get your power back on. These type of faults often require extensive work and specialist teams, such as tree cutters, which is why the restoration time seems so far away. If we can restore power sooner, we promise we will do so.

If you need more information, please send us a message on Whatsapp or call us on 105 and quote reference 'SZ8593'

Faults.name: HP13 Area

Faults.message:
We're sorry about the power cut in the area. The power cut is affecting some properties in the area shown on the map, but not all. This is why you may notice some of your neighbours have power, but you don't. It is normal to see 1 in 3 homes affected. We are working with our engineers to fix the problem as quickly as possible, and well keep you updated here on PowerTrack.

It's busier than usual just now, but we'll be sending our engineers to the area as soon as possible. When they're on their way, we'll let you know what time they'll be arriving. At this stage, we're aiming to have your power back on by.

The cause of this power cut is a fault on one of the cables that come into your home. For some customers, you will have a complete power cut, for others, you may notice just some of your power or appliances not working. This is a common type of fault we see, and our teams are very experienced in dealing with these issues. In most cases, our engineers are able to restore power by simply replacing a fuse at the local substation, however, if the fault is severe, this may result in us needing to make full repairs, which can take longer. If this does happen, we will let you know.

If you need more information, please send us a message on Whatsapp or call us on 105 and quote reference 'SZ8671'
